text.title: Azure NAT Gateway for Outbound Connectivity Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Azure NAT Gateway for Outbound Connectivity` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Public IP count MUST be sized at ≥ 1 IP per 50,000 expected concurrent outbound connections.
rule.rationale: Each IP gives ~64k SNAT ports; under-sizing causes silent connection drops.

rule.statement: Public IPs MUST be Standard SKU with `zones = ["1","2","3"]` for zone redundancy. Basic SKU is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Zone-redundant IP survives AZ failure; Basic SKU is incompatible with NAT Gateway.

rule.statement: `idle_timeout_in_minutes` MUST be tuned to workload shape (4-15 min). 120-minute default is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Long idle timeouts hold SNAT ports unnecessarily; exhausts capacity at scale.

rule.statement: Diagnostic logs MUST be enabled and `SNATConnectionCount`, `DroppedPackets` alerts MUST fire at ≥ 80% utilisation.
rule.rationale: SNAT exhaustion presents as silent drops; proactive alerts are the only early warning.

rule.statement: Workloads requiring > 16 IPs MUST use a Public IP Prefix instead of individual IPs.
rule.rationale: Above 16 individual IPs the NAT Gateway limit is hit; prefix gives contiguous block.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
